$comment: Cross-repo lockstep pin (spec 006). Binds the acme-vue-encore generator + module catalog to the template-encore baseline structure and to the frozen app-invariant spec hashes (the `encore-app-architecture` and `security-data-invariants` invariants). The invariant-hash pin was deferred through Phase 1 and Phase 2 (`encore-app-architecture` absorbed the static-serving wiring from spec 004 in Phase 2, so pinning its hash earlier would have locked a value about to change) and is now ACTIVE: the Phase 3 handshake flipped invariantPin.status to 'pinned', filled the invariant spec.md SHA-256 content hashes, and bumped pinnedRef to the finalized template-encore main. Re-hash drift on either invariant now fails the gate (FR-002). pinnedRef was refreshed from b37d3d7 to c7603ee (template-encore main) once the intervening commits were verified to touch no pinned unit: the invariant spec.md hashes re-hash identical, the core services are unchanged. A future ref or hash bump remains a deliberate, coupling-gated edit to spec 006. Note: invariantPin.specs and hashes keep template-encore's current numbered paths (specs/001-..., specs/002-...) because the checker fetches those exact paths at pinnedRef; when template-encore renumbers its own corpus, repointing these paths is part of that same coupling-gated bump.

invariantPin.reason: Phase 3 handshake (2026-06-23): both invariants finalized in template-encore at b37d3d7; pin advanced to c7603ee (main) on 2026-06-24 with both hashes re-verified identical. Each value is the SHA-256 of the invariant spec.md at the pinned ref (matching check.ts sha256File); a re-hash mismatch is reported as DRIFT and fails the gate.
